data.title: Eval Scaffold Generator with TODO Commit Guard
data.description:
## Context

DSO skill authors (both human and agent) currently have no automated way to scaffold eval configs when modifying skills. The project ships 3 representative promptfoo evals out of ~30 skills. Agents making skill changes must author promptfoo configs from scratch or copy-paste from examples — leading to inconsistent structure and risk of shipping placeholder evals that pass vacuously. This epic adds a generator that produces structurally valid eval scaffolds from SKILL.md files, plus a commit-time guard that blocks incomplete (TODO-marked) evals from being committed. The result: any agent modifying a skill can generate a scaffold, fill in real assertions as part of a RED eval workflow, and be mechanically prevented from committing placeholders.

## Success Criteria

1. A generate-skill-eval.sh script accepts a skill name, reads the skill's SKILL.md description block (first H2 section or frontmatter description field, whichever is present; fence-aware H2 extraction that skips code blocks; exits non-zero with helpful message if neither exists), and produces an evals/promptfooconfig.yaml in the skill's directory with the correct provider/grader boilerplate, 1-2 test cases with TODO-marked llm-rubric assertions derived from the extracted description, and inline comments referencing the skill's purpose. The generator exits non-zero if the skill directory doesn't exist or already has an evals/promptfooconfig.yaml (no silent overwrites). Uses atomic writes (temp file + mv) to prevent partial output. YAML output escapes special characters from SKILL.md content. Out of scope: --force and --dry-run flags.
2. A commit-time guard implemented as a new code block in record-test-status.sh (safeguard file — requires user approval per CLAUDE.md rule 18) scans all staged evals/promptfooconfig.yaml files for TODO markers and blocks the commit with a clear message identifying which file(s) contain placeholders. The guard runs unconditionally as a static text scan — it is not gated on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and has no fail-open paths. The guard also rejects configs with an empty tests: list (zero test cases) and requires at least one llm-rubric assertion type (prevents trivial structural-only assertions). Assertion quality beyond type enforcement is caught by the existing review gate (eval configs are not in the review-gate allowlist).
3. SKILL-EVALS-GUIDE.md enables a skill author to (a) run generate-skill-eval.sh and produce a valid scaffold, (b) replace all TODOs with llm-rubric assertions following the documented convention, and (c) understand why the commit guard blocks a TODO-marked eval — all without reading source code. Verified by: guide contains a complete worked example showing the generate-then-fill-in-then-commit cycle.
4. Eval coverage increases from 3/~30 skills (baseline at epic start) to at least 8/~30 skills by epic close, with each new eval containing at least one llm-rubric assertion that exercises a named behavioral property of the skill (not just "output is not empty").

## Scenario Analysis

Round 1 (6 surviving):
- **TODO-marked assertions pass YAML validation** (high): run-skill-evals.sh only checks top-level keys. Guard must run before eval runner.
- **Generator overwrites existing hand-authored evals** (critical): 3 existing configs at risk. Generator must detect and refuse.
- **TODO guard is the only hard gate** (critical): Eval failures are non-blocking at commit time. Guard must be zero-tolerance with no fail-open paths.
- **Empty tests list passes validation** (high): Guard must also validate non-empty test count.
- **Review gate not configured for evals/** (medium): Accepted tradeoff — evals contain behavioral assertions that warrant review.
- **.test-index registration needed** (medium): Generator script needs .test-index entry for TDD enforcement.

Round 2 (6 surviving):
- **SKILL.md parsing: no H2 and no frontmatter** (high): Generator must handle gracefully — exit non-zero with helpful message.
- **H2 extraction in code blocks** (medium): Fence-aware parsing required to avoid premature truncation.
- **YAML special character escaping** (high): Shell interpolation must escape colons, quotes, newlines from SKILL.md content.
- **Guard scope ambiguity** (high): Guard scans staged eval configs only (via git diff --cached). An unstaged TODO config does not block — this is the correct behavior since the generator-then-fill-in workflow is branch-local.
- **Partial write failure** (medium): Generator must use atomic writes (temp file + mv).
- **Assertion substance vs. presence** (medium): Guard requires llm-rubric type; review gate provides deeper quality check.

## Constraints

- record-test-status.sh is a safeguard file (CLAUDE.md rule 18) — modifications require explicit user approval before implementation begins.

## Dependencies

- 7944-dcc5 (Promptfoo-Based Skills Eval Framework) — completed

## Approach

Two-piece design: a shell script generator that parses SKILL.md for behavioral description (fence-aware, with YAML escaping and atomic writes) and produces a starter promptfooconfig.yaml with TODO-marked llm-rubric assertions, plus a commit-time guard in record-test-status.sh that scans staged eval configs and blocks on TODO markers, empty test lists, or missing llm-rubric assertions. The guard runs as a static text scan independent of the eval runner and API key availability.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: re-triggered via gate
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 12 (6 round 1 + 6 round 2)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: scenario analysis re-run 1 time(s)
